meaning: adj.不平等的,不同的,不平衡的,不胜任的
examples: These unequal treaties were made under duress.
This is ambitious in any circumstances, and in a divided and unequal society the two ideals can clash outright.
The two unequal shorter sides of the curvilinear trapezoid of each building change their geometry every two floors.
The fight between the two camps has become increasingly unequal, diplomats and analysts said.
But many situations, Mr. Chwe points out, involve parties with unequal levels of strategic thinking.
